boundary_rules.description: Regime runtime layers must use Protocol/Adapter/Gateway abstractions instead of macro implementation imports.
Strategy must not import simulated_trading ORM models directly.
Simulated trading must not import strategy ORM models directly.
Events must not import downstream handlers or ORM models directly.
boundary_rules.rationale: Delegate via EventSubscriberRegistry and downstream repositories/gateways.

boundary_rules.description: Account interface must not import simulated_trading ORM models or migration commands directly.
boundary_rules.rationale: Route cross-module reads through account-owned repositories or gateways.

boundary_rules.description: Application layers must use other apps' application facades instead of importing their infrastructure packages.
boundary_rules.rationale: Cross-app concrete dependencies create hidden coupling; expose application-level facades/providers from the owning app.

audit_rules.description: Application layers must not import infrastructure models directly, including dynamic import escapes.
audit_rules.rationale: Move ORM access behind repositories or adapters.

audit_rules.description: Application layers must not access ORM managers directly.
audit_rules.rationale: Application code should coordinate repositories instead of talking to ORM managers.

audit_rules.description: Application layers must not own Django transaction scopes or resolve ORM models dynamically.
audit_rules.rationale: Transaction scope and runtime ORM model resolution belong in infrastructure, admin, or migrations instead of application orchestration.

audit_rules.description: Application layers must not invoke Django management commands directly outside whitelisted composition entrypoints.
audit_rules.rationale: Application orchestration should depend on use cases and repositories rather than shelling out through management commands.

audit_rules.description: Application layers must not introduce pandas or numpy imports; data-frame and vectorized work belongs in infrastructure adapters/services.
audit_rules.rationale: Keep application code focused on orchestration and keep heavy data processing behind infrastructure boundaries.

audit_rules.description: Retired shared business compatibility modules must not be imported anywhere.
audit_rules.rationale: Business config/model compatibility shims and asset-analysis registry moved back to owning apps and must not be reintroduced through shared/.

audit_rules.description: Django AppConfig definitions must live in app-root apps.py only.
audit_rules.rationale: Layer packages must not double as Django app configuration modules.

audit_rules.description: Interface layers must call application services, not infrastructure packages directly.
audit_rules.rationale: Interface should stay at validation and response formatting boundaries.

audit_rules.description: Only Django admin entrypoints may import app-root models.py shims.
audit_rules.rationale: App-root models.py files are Django bootstrap shims, not general-purpose import surfaces for business code.

audit_rules.description: Core application code must not directly depend on app infrastructure packages or ORM managers.
audit_rules.rationale: core/application should remain project orchestration, not an extra business module.

audit_rules.description: Core controller entrypoints must not import app infrastructure packages or touch ORM managers directly.
audit_rules.rationale: Core controllers should delegate to app application services.

audit_rules.description: Shared code must not depend on business apps.
audit_rules.rationale: shared/ must remain a pure technical component layer.

audit_rules.description: Shared must not define Django ORM models.
audit_rules.rationale: Business models belong to their owning app, not shared/.

audit_rules.description: Application layers should depend on injected protocols or facades, not concrete infrastructure repository modules.
audit_rules.rationale: Keep application orchestration independent from concrete repository module imports.

audit_rules.description: Application layers should route same-app concrete infrastructure access through repository_provider.py or other application facades.
audit_rules.rationale: Keep use_cases/tasks/services focused on orchestration while composition stays in repository_provider.py.

audit_rules.description: apps/ and core/ code must use timezone-aware datetime APIs instead of bare datetime.now()/utcnow().
audit_rules.rationale: Use datetime.now(timezone.utc), datetime.now(UTC), or django.utils.timezone.now() to respect USE_TZ=True.

audit_rules.description: Macro should not depend on account module runtime configuration or ORM.
audit_rules.rationale: Global runtime settings should move behind config_center, not account.

audit_rules.description: Data center should use provider registries and protocols instead of importing business app infrastructure implementations.
audit_rules.rationale: Owning apps should register providers; data_center must not reverse-import their implementation packages.

audit_rules.description: Domain layers must not import application or infrastructure packages, including dynamic import escapes.
audit_rules.rationale: Domain code must stay pure and depend only on domain abstractions and standard-library constructs.

regime.note: Runtime access to macro is mediated by regime-owned abstractions.

macro.note: Macro sync is invoked through regime orchestration boundaries.

events.note: Event fan-out should stop at registry/repository boundaries.

strategy.note: Strategy should not reach simulated_trading ORM directly.

simulated_trading.note: Simulated trading should not reach strategy ORM directly.

dashboard.note: Dashboard keeps cross-module reads in query/DTO boundaries.

account.note: Interface reads from account-owned repositories instead of simulated_trading ORM imports.
